Apple___Apple_scab.symptoms: Olive-green to black spots on leaves and fruit. Leaves may curl and drop early.

treatment.precautions: Wear gloves and mask. Do not spray near water bodies. PHI: 7 days.
Avoid inhalation. Do not mix with alkaline pesticides. PHI: 21 days.

Apple___Apple_scab.cultural_practices: Prune infected branches. Collect and destroy fallen leaves. Improve air circulation.

Apple___Black_rot.symptoms: Brown rotting lesions on fruit. Purple spots on leaves with frog-eye appearance.

treatment.precautions: Resistance may develop with repeated use. Rotate fungicides. PHI: 7 days.
Phytotoxic on sensitive varieties. Avoid in hot weather. PHI: 14 days.

Apple___Black_rot.cultural_practices: Remove mummified fruit. Prune dead wood. Sterilize pruning tools.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surface. Tube-like structures on leaf undersides.

treatment.frequency: Apply at pink bud stage, repeat every 7-10 days
treatment.precautions: Do not use near water. Keep away from children. PHI: 7 days.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.cultural_practices: Remove nearby juniper/cedar trees. Plant resistant apple varieties.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.symptoms: Rectangular gray to tan lesions parallel to leaf veins. Lesions have yellow halos.

treatment.precautions: Rotate mode of action to prevent resistance. PHI: 7 days.
Wear protective clothing. Avoid drift. PHI: 14 days.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.cultural_practices: Crop rotation. Tillage to reduce residue. Plant resistant hybrids.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.symptoms: Brick-red to brown powdery pustules on both leaf surfaces. Leaves turn yellow.

treatment.precautions: Do not apply during pollination. PHI: 21 days.

treatment.frequency: Apply at first sign of disease
treatment.precautions: Highly toxic to aquatic organisms. PHI: 7 days.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.cultural_practices: Early planting. Use resistant varieties. Balanced fertilization.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.symptoms: Long, cigar-shaped gray-green to tan lesions (2-6 inches). Lesions appear water-soaked initially.

treatment.precautions: Eye and skin irritant. Use full PPE. PHI: 14 days.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.cultural_practices: Rotate crops. Till infected debris. Plant tolerant hybrids.

Grape___Black_rot.symptoms: Circular tan spots with dark borders on leaves. Black mummified berries.

treatment.precautions: Harmful if swallowed. Wash hands after use. PHI: 14 days.
Irritating to eyes and skin. Avoid contact. PHI: 7 days.

Grape___Black_rot.cultural_practices: Remove mummified clusters. Prune for air circulation. Destroy infected material.

Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings (target-board pattern) on older leaves.

treatment.precautions: Wear mask during application. Avoid during windy conditions. PHI: 7 days.
Do not enter treated field for 12 hours. PHI: 14 days.

Potato___Early_blight.cultural_practices: Crop rotation (3 years). Use certified seed. Avoid overhead irrigation.

Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Water-soaked, pale green lesions turning dark brown with white mold on underside.

treatment.precautions: CRITICAL: Act fast - disease spreads rapidly. PHI: 14 days.
Use full protective gear. Highly toxic. PHI: 21 days.

Potato___Late_blight.cultural_practices: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid overhead irrigation. Destroy infected tubers.

Tomato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark concentric ring lesions on lower leaves. Yellow halo around spots. Stem lesions.

treatment.precautions: Copper accumulation in soil with repeated use. PHI: 7 days.
Rotate fungicides. May cause phytotoxicity on stressed plants. PHI: 3 days.

Tomato___Early_blight.cultural_practices: Stake plants for air flow. Mulch to reduce splash. Remove lower infected leaves.

Tomato___Late_blight.symptoms: Greasy, water-soaked lesions on leaves and stems. White mold in humid conditions.

treatment.precautions: Very fast-spreading disease. Begin treatment at first symptoms. PHI: 14 days.

Tomato___Late_blight.cultural_practices: Avoid wetting foliage. Destroy crop debris. Use resistant varieties.

Tomato___Leaf_Miner.symptoms: Serpentine mines on leaves. Silvery blotches. Larvae inside leaf tissue.

treatment.precautions: Do not apply when bees are active. PHI: 3 days.
Low toxicity to mammals. Avoid spray on flowers. PHI: 1 day.

Tomato___Leaf_Miner.cultural_practices: Use yellow sticky traps. Remove infested leaves. Encourage natural predators.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptoms: Yellowing and upward curling of young leaves. Stunted growth. Flower drop.

treatment.frequency: Preventive application every 15 days for whitefly control
treatment.precautions: No direct cure for virus. Target whitefly vector. PHI: 7 days.
Extremely toxic to bees. Do not spray during flowering. PHI: 3 days.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.cultural_practices: Use reflective mulch to repel whiteflies. Remove infected plants. Use resistant varieties.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves with yellow halos. Lesions on fruit.

treatment.dosage: 3.0 g + 0.2 g per litre of water
treatment.precautions: Copper resistance may develop. Rotate bactericides. PHI: 14 days.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.cultural_practices: Use disease-free seed. Avoid overhead irrigation. Remove infected plants promptly.

Rice___Brown_Spot.symptoms: Oval to circular brown spots on leaves with gray centers. Grain discoloration.

treatment.frequency: Apply at booting and heading stage
treatment.precautions: Wear protective gear. PHI: 21 days before harvest.

treatment.frequency: Apply at first sign of disease
treatment.precautions: Avoid contact with skin. PHI: 14 days.

Rice___Brown_Spot.cultural_practices: Balanced fertilization (avoid excess N). Proper water management. Certified seed.

Wheat___Leaf_rust.symptoms: Orange-red pustules on upper leaf surface. Pustules rupture releasing spores.

treatment.precautions: Do not apply during grain fill. PHI: 35 days.
Toxic to aquatic organisms. PHI: 35 days.

Wheat___Leaf_rust.cultural_practices: Plant resistant varieties. Timely sowing. Avoid late planting.

healthy.symptoms: Plant appears healthy with no visible disease symptoms.
healthy.cultural_practices: Continue regular monitoring. Maintain proper fertilization, irrigation and pest management as preventive measures.
